title: Gourmet Vietnamese Tofu Canh Chua
description: A cultured, aerated midnight craving soup featuring harissa spice and a tangy tamarind broth.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: cut into 1 inch cubes and pat dry

ingredients.name: harissa paste
ingredients.notes: choose a mild or medium heat level

instructions: Cube the tofu and pat dry with paper towels.
Heat a fondue pot over medium heat and add the oil, then sauté the bell pepper and zucchini until slightly softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and cook for another 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a gentle boil.
Stir in the tamarind paste, harissa paste, soy sauce, and lime juice until the broth takes on a bright, tangy color.
Gently fold in the tofu cubes and simmer for 8 minutes until the tofu is heated through.
Add the fresh basil leaves and cook for an additional 1 minute to release their aroma.
Taste the soup and season with salt or pepper if desired, though the soy sauce and tamarind are usually sufficient.
Ladle the soup into individual fondue pots and serve immediately with crusty bread or rice.
